name: Upper Woodford
coordsSource: web-verified:listing-centroid(51.13674,-1.82210);getthedata SP4-6PA Upper Meads(51.136208,-1.822106 ~70m); cross-check SP4-6NU nr Bridge Inn 51.1316,-1.8242 & historic-england 37-38 Upper Woodford 51.1294,-1.823; stored 51.147,-1.7823 ~3km NE rejected; 2026-06-04
overview: Upper Woodford is the largest of the three villages making up Woodford civil parish, on the west bank of the Salisbury Avon about 6 miles north of Salisbury (the parish population was 443 at the 2011 census). The River Avon runs alongside the village road, and a bridge here - recorded as far back as 1370 - links Upper Woodford to the villages on the east bank. The riverside Bridge Inn is the village's pub.
character: A quiet, green riverside village of cottages and larger houses set among water meadows, with the Avon a constant presence beside the lane.
amenities: Woodford Valley village hall at neighbouring Middle Woodford
Woodford Valley CE Primary Academy at Middle Woodford
Salisbury Reds bus service through the valley
No village shop or supermarket - everyday shopping is in Salisbury

commutes.notes: About 6 miles south down the valley road for shops, services and the station
From Salisbury on South Western Railway; fastest services about 1h 20m
Salisbury Reds runs through the Woodford Valley villages

prices.summary: Stock is mostly period and detached riverside houses, thatched cottages and converted farm buildings, with very low transaction volumes given the small size of the village; the Avon-side setting and Salisbury proximity support premium values. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Visiting nearby Heale Garden at Heale House, between Middle and Upper Woodford, where Charles II sheltered in 1651
Riverside walks and fly-fishing along the Salisbury Avon
Crossing the historic Avon bridge to walk in the wider valley
placesToEat: The Bridge Inn, Upper Woodford (riverside pub and food)
The Wheatsheaf Inn at Lower Woodford
pros: Attractive riverside setting as the largest and best-served of the Woodford villages, with its own pub
Well-regarded village Church of England primary academy nearby
About 6 miles from Salisbury's full range of shops, services and rail links
cons: No shop or supermarket in the village, so a car is essential
Limited bus frequency compared with town living
Some riverside properties sit close to the Avon flood plain
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a picturesque, slightly better-served riverside valley village with a pub, within easy reach of Salisbury.

sources.label: British History Online - VCH Wiltshire, Woodford
Ofsted - Woodford Valley CE Primary Academy (URN 138188, Good, Jun 2025)
